title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to Gotham FC in their NWSL debut at Gillette Stadium.
The match set a record with eight yellow cards, including a red card for Legacy's Bianca St-Georges.
Over 30,000 fans attended, marking a new high for women's soccer in Boston.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall at Fenway on March 13, 2026, kicking off St. Patrick's Day weekend.
The concert featured an opening lineup of Boston-based punk bands: The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown.
The band debuted their new EP 'New England Forever,' including the protest song 'Citizen I.C.E.' in response to ICE actions.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Bennett was diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at age 11 and credits Joslin Diabetes Center for his ongoing support.
He aims to inspire others and raise awareness for Type 1 Diabetes through his marathon run.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter, a Springfield native, earns her fifth Oscar nomination for 'Sinners', becoming the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history.
Carter was the first Black person to win the Oscar for Best Costume Design in 2019 and first to win multiple Oscars in any category in 2023.
Carter's costume designs in 'Sinners' involved Massachusetts resources, including Brimfield Antique Flea Marketplace and local Boston manufacturers.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 layoffs if a $18.5 million tax override isn’t approved by voters.
Proposed cuts include reduced course offerings, after-school fees, and increased class sizes up to 30 students.
Voting on the property-tax override, crucial for funding, is scheduled for May 5 during the town election.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Massachusetts PETS Act proposes banning sales of dogs and cats in pet shops to reduce reliance on puppy mills.
The bill would allow pet shops to partner with shelters for adoptions and promote responsible pet ownership.
It addresses housing and insurance barriers, expanding pet access in public housing and preventing breed-based insurance denial.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: State officials urge water conservation as drought intensifies despite heavy snowfall in February.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts elevated to Level 3 Critical Drought; Western Massachusetts at Level 1 Mild Drought.
Residents advised to conserve water through efficient appliances and mindful usage amid ongoing drought.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph's essay discusses why some straight women obscure their boyfriends on Instagram.
Joseph questions traditional heterosexual norms, suggesting women are rethinking centering men in their lives.
The essay sparked widespread debate online and inspired discussion on modern relationships and norms.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O’Neill is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp for children with medical conditions.
His motivation is personal; his fiancée's sister, Reagan Carey, benefited from the camp during her battle with bone cancer.
O’Neill aims to raise donations to help the camp provide free, fun, and medically safe experiences for kids in need.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles will not trade wide receiver A.J. Brown to the Patriots at this time, according to reports.
Trade discussions may resume around June 1 when salary cap relief is available for the Eagles.
The Patriots reportedly offered first- and third-round picks for A.J. Brown, which the Eagles denied.
